input.user_input: How does the UCC Article 2 battle of the forms work when two parties exchange conflicting standard terms?

actual:
The "battle of the forms" under Article 2 of the Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) addresses the common commercial scenario where buyers and sellers exchange standard order forms (like purchase orders and invoices) that contain conflicting or additional terms. 

UCC § 2-207 was drafted to abolish the strict common law "mirror image rule," which held that an acceptance must exactly match the offer, and the "last shot rule," which typically bound the parties to the terms of the last form sent before performance.
